coverage_gaps.gap_topic: Tauri+React+ReactFlow tech stack Decision-Review not formally recorded
coverage_gaps.why_missing: Search 4 shows extensive facts about Tauri/React/ReactFlow being adopted (user confirmed switch to independent desktop app, scaffolding set up, first compile successful), but Search 5 shows no Decision-Review PENDING specifically for this frontend tech stack selection. The old Svelte Decision-Review was expired (invalid_at: 2026-03-15), yet no replacement Decision-Review was created for the Tauri+React+ReactFlow choice. The decision was made and executed but lacks the mandatory [Decision-Review] audit trail.

Search 7 shows shadcn/ui was recommended by 6-agent research and is being used with TailwindCSS v4. However, the facts linking shadcn/ui to React are expired (invalid_at: 2026-03-17T09:39:53). The replacement fact says 'Obsidian components are replaced by shadcn/ui' but there is no Decision-Review or formal validation that shadcn/ui components work correctly within the Tauri+React context for all 14 MVP requirements. 87 shadcn components exist in Pencil design but no verification they map to actual implementation needs.

Search 1 shows 'Frontend-first + Backend sync' approach adopted, and 'verification_graph/state_graph/react_agent decouple from frontend'. However, there are no facts describing the actual API contract between the new Tauri frontend and the existing Python backend. The backend was built for the old Obsidian plugin frontend. No facts about REST/WebSocket/IPC protocol decisions for Tauri-to-backend communication.

coverage_gaps.gap_topic: Claude Code CLI spawning integration - no implementation details
coverage_gaps.why_missing: Search 7 shows 'independent desktop application will integrate by spawning the Claude Code CLI' but there are zero facts about: (1) how the CLI process lifecycle is managed, (2) stdin/stdout parsing protocol, (3) error handling for CLI failures, (4) how CLI output maps to UI state. This is a core architectural dependency with no design depth.

coverage_gaps.gap_topic: Local JSON storage design lacks specification
coverage_gaps.why_missing: Search 7 confirms 'independent desktop application uses local JSON for data storage' but no facts describe: (1) JSON schema for whiteboard files, (2) concurrent access handling, (3) data migration from old format, (4) backup/recovery strategy, (5) file watching for external edits. Only one fact mentions 'WhiteboardFile' type defined in types.ts but no schema validation design.

Search 1 shows 'old frontend replaced by new frontend' and Search 7 shows 'Obsidian components replaced by shadcn/ui'. But the git status shows massive deletions in obsidian-canvas-learning/ (80+ files deleted). No facts document: (1) whether existing Obsidian users need a migration path, (2) whether the Obsidian plugin is fully deprecated or maintained in parallel, (3) what happens to the canvas-progress-tracker/obsidian-plugin/ code.

coverage_gaps.gap_topic: 5 PENDING Decision-Reviews from PRD validation unresolved
coverage_gaps.why_missing: Search 5 reveals '5 PENDING Decision-Review items requiring independent validation sessions' (fact from PRD-Review). Specific pending dimensions include: (1) retrieval layer timing issues, (2) UX impact of status prompts, (3) Mode D image format/size limitations. These predate the Tauri pivot and may need re-evaluation in the new architecture context.

coverage_gaps.gap_topic: ChatPanel and Edge dialog implementation complexity in new stack
coverage_gaps.why_missing: Search 6 identifies ChatPanel and Edge dialog as MVP items with 'significant difference in implementation complexity' between old and new stacks. The fact notes the complexity comparison was done for Svelte4 vs pure-TS approaches, but no updated analysis exists for the React+ReactFlow implementation path now being used.

coverage_gaps.gap_topic: Testing strategy for Tauri desktop app undefined
coverage_gaps.why_missing: No facts across any of the 7 searches mention testing approaches for the new Tauri+React frontend. DD-07 requires adversarial testing with real data, but there is no mention of: (1) component testing framework (Vitest? Jest?), (2) E2E testing for desktop app (Playwright? WebDriver?), (3) Tauri-specific integration testing approach.

coverage_gaps.gap_topic: Performance benchmarks for ReactFlow with large whiteboards missing
coverage_gaps.why_missing: ReactFlow canvas is confirmed working (version 12.8.2, first compile successful), but no facts address performance with large node counts. The existing exam whiteboard design mentions node limits as a gap ('Gap4 node count unlimited still needs resolution'). No benchmarking or performance requirements defined for the new ReactFlow implementation.

Search 7 shows successful Windows compilation (canvas-learning-frontend.exe) with VS Build Tools and Rustup setup. But no facts cover: (1) macOS/Linux build pipeline, (2) auto-update mechanism, (3) code signing, (4) distribution channel (GitHub releases? custom updater?). The user's platform is Windows 11 but DD-06 originally required cross-platform consideration.

coverage_gaps.gap_topic: Accessibility and theme compatibility for new UI
coverage_gaps.why_missing: The old Obsidian plugin had strict CSS rules (DD-06: CSS variables for colors, theme compatibility, no hardcoded colors). The new shadcn/ui + TailwindCSS stack has different theming patterns. No facts document accessibility requirements or dark/light theme strategy for the standalone Tauri app.

existing_context_nodes: Tauri 2.0 - confirmed as desktop shell, first compile successful
React - confirmed UI framework for independent desktop app
ReactFlow (@xyflow/react v12.8.2) - whiteboard engine, integrated and running
shadcn/ui - recommended by 6-agent research, replacing Obsidian components
TailwindCSS v4 - configured in frontend/ directory, part of validated stack
Local JSON storage - confirmed as data persistence layer
Claude Code CLI spawning - confirmed integration method
Frontend-first + Backend sync - adopted approach
5-Epic plan - based on confirmed tech selection and 14 MVP requirements
14 MVP core requirements - confirmed, architecture document based on these
2 underlying systems - confirmed as part of MVP essentials
87 shadcn components in Pencil design file
canvas-learning-frontend.exe - first successful compilation
WhiteboardFile type - defined in frontend/src/types.ts
App.tsx - contains ReactFlow canvas as basic layout
verification_graph/state_graph/react_agent - decoupled from frontend
Edge dialog - MVP requirement with complexity variance across stacks
ChatPanel - MVP requirement with complexity variance across stacks
Dashboard - MVP requirement with 3 core functions (whiteboard list, exam whiteboard list, 3 action buttons)
Decision-Review PENDING dimensions - retrieval timing, UX status prompts, Mode D image limits

summary.key_finding: The project underwent a major architectural pivot from Obsidian plugin (Svelte) to independent Tauri desktop app (React+ReactFlow) around 2026-03-17. While the tech stack has been selected and scaffolding validated, critical gaps exist in: (1) no formal Decision-Review for the new stack, (2) undefined API contract between Tauri frontend and Python backend, (3) unspecified Claude Code CLI integration protocol, and (4) unresolved pre-pivot PENDING decisions that need re-evaluation.
